violations.text: Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

Various foods in walk-in cooler were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods (shrimp) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (vegetables) in the reach-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, containers, rice cookers, cutting board) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

All prepared foods throughout the establishment were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package in the front kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. 1. tables 2. refrigeration units 3. hoods 4. shelving throughout kitchen 5. front hand sink New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served (PIC has not yet registered for a course at time of inspection). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The food prep sink is lacking a proper air gap (air break observed). A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/29/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

A container with a large spoon were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as chicken) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

All prepared foods throughout the establishment were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

Thermometers are lacking in refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Knives/Cleavers were observed stored in unclean space between prep unit and prep table. In use utensils must be stored in clean dry locations between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

1) Several meat cleavers were observed in poor repair (heavily chipped and damaged). PIC removed all cleavers from use 2) The left foot pedal of the hand wash sink in the kitchen was observed to leak when in use while the right foot pedal did not dispense water at all Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

A plastic jug was observed cut in half and being used as a scoop. Equipment and utensils must be designed to be durable and easily cleanable. PIC instructed to discontinue use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2024

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed with an accumulation of debris: 1) All refrigeration units (including prep units) both inside and out had a heavy accumulation of food and soil residue 2) All food prep and equipment counter tops/tabletops had an accumulation of food and soil residue 3) All food and equipment storage racks had an accumulation of food and soil residue 4) Storage shelves under area holding hot holding kettles had an accumulation of old rodent droppings 5) All rice cookers, soup, and sauce hot holding kettles had an accumulation of food debris Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

The food prep sink is lacking a proper air gap (air break observed). A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap or installing an approved backflow prevention device. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024
Plumbing on a wall near the wok line was in poor repair (observed leaking into a container and wrapped in plastic). New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The condensate from the ceiling vent in the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into containers. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/28/2024

A knife (chipped) and the wok brushes (frayed) at the line were observed in poor repair. PIC removed utensils/equipment from kitchen area to be replaced/serviced/repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following were observed to have an accumulation of food residue and other debris at the time of inspection: 1) Refrigeration units (internal and external surfaces) 2) Cook Line equipment (Fryers, Wok Line, Smoker Box) 3) Prep Tables New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2023

An unknown condensate line located in the back prep area ceiling is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2023

The walls near the hot holding line and ware-washing area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the prep/dry storage area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 10/03/2023
